comment: Golden fixtures for the Ruby port of convex/moderation.ts. extraRules/extraLists are SYNTHETIC (marked synthetic:true, names prefixed x_) — they exist to exercise evaluator paths the seed data never reaches (hybrid_vision, office rules, uncovered operators). They are still executed by the REAL TS evaluators in scripts/golden/generate.ts. Each fixture carries an `expect` block that the generator validates to guarantee the fixture is not degenerate.

extraRules.name: x_op_matches_invalid
extraRules.comment: invalid regex: matches must rescue and return false

extraRules.name: x_op_eq_float
extraRules.comment: listing carries bedrooms 5.0 — JS String(5.0) === '5' must equal String(5)

extraRules.name: x_op_lt_nan
extraRules.comment: Number(title) is NaN — NaN < 5 must be false, rule never fires

extraRules.name: x_office_ids
extraRules.comment: numeric id in config vs string office on listing — String() normalization

extraRules.name: x_list_mixed
extraRules.comment: listRef with one exact and one regex item, resolved via resolveListRefs

extraRules.name: x_country_fullname
extraRules.comment: filter uses the full country name, listing uses the ISO code

extraRules.name: x_uk_rule
extraRules.comment: filter GB, listing UK — normCountryCode alias

extraRules.name: x_tie_a
extraRules.comment: same priority as x_tie_b — stable sort must keep definition order

fixtures.label: ops_full_matrix

fixtures.label: ops_missing_fields
fixtures.note: missing fields only satisfy empty/is_null; not_in and is_false on missing fields are FALSE
